mission.title: flux: Flux CLI tool doesn't respect context namespace
mission.description: Flux CLI tool doesn't respect context namespace. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
### Describe the bug

Say a Kubeconfig has the following block:

```
contexts:
- context:
    cluster: cluster
    namespace: services
    user: user
  name: cluster
current-context: cluster
```

When you run flux commands it will not target the

steps.title: Apply the fix for Flux CLI tool doesn't respect context namespace
steps.description:
I agree with this report in principle, however I'd like to note as a point of information that this is not a regression. This is how `flux` CLI has always worked. You can use the `FLUX_SYSTEM_NAMESPACE` environment variable to set `flux` CLI to work in a different namespace, but, generally, it is
```yaml
contexts:
- context:
    cluster: cluster
    namespace: services
    user: user
  name: cluster
current-context: cluster
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Flux CLI tool doesn't respect context namespace" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I agree with this report in principle, however I'd like to note as a point of information that this is not a regression. This is how `flux` CLI has always worked.
codeSnippets:
contexts:
- context:
    cluster: cluster
    namespace: services
    user: user
  name: cluster
current-context: cluster

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
